Lack of sleep can lead to higher cholesterol and blood pressure. Sleep deprivation can lower levels of leptin, a hormone that stabilises metabolism and appetite. This can also lead to obesity, if left unchecked. Sleep deprivation has been found to raise levels of ghrelin, the hunger hormone, and decrease levels of leptin, the satiety hormone. That’s why people seek relief in foods that raise blood sugar. The American Academy of Sleep Medicine recommends that adults should get a minimum of seven to eight hours of sleep each night. Staying up late can lead to consuming unhealthy snacks and junk food high in carbs and sugar. All this increases the risk of Type 2 diabetes and is equally connected to obesity.

Just like any other routine, having a definite sleeping pattern can help a person maintain a cycle of rest and activity. Sleeping at the same time every day and waking up on time will encourage quality sleep. Proper relaxation of body and mind before sleeping means that one should keep the mobile phone aside for at least half an hour before bed. It helps if the room is dark and quiet. Plus, one can always read a good book or listen to soothing music which can lull one into deep sleep.

question: Working from home requires that you may have to put your _______ life on _______. Some people find this very _______ as they like to keep their work and home lives separate.
solution: Many find this quite _______ as they prefer to keep their work and home lives separate. -> Prefer to keep work & home life separate -> first 2 blanks = something that DOES NOT allow that separation Option 1: Put life on camera -> Doesn’t make sense -> Eliminated (the verb should be ‘have’ instead of 'put' for camera to make sense) Option 3: Put life on video -> Doesn’t make sense -> Eliminated (same as option 1) Option 4: Put life on microscope -> Doesn’t make sense -> Eliminated (the conjunction should be ‘under’ instead of ‘on' for microscope to make sense) Option 2: All words make sense -> Correct answer!
option1: entire; camera; complicated
option2: private; screen; disconcerting
option3: actual; video; uncomfortable
option4: family; microscope; disturbing

question: In its early stages, dementia is often _______ from normal ageing, in which the older brain may naturally begin to show signs of cognitive impairment. Neither dementia nor cognitive ageing is currently _______, though they may be slowed through diet, exercise, and keeping the mind agile with social and intellectual activity.
solution: In its early stages, dementia is often _______ from normal aging, and (neither dementia nor cognitive ageing) is currently _______, -> 2 Cases. Case 1: Dementia = viewed different than normal aging + it is curable. Case 2: Dementia = not viewed different, + incurable. Note: (it cant be a mix of both since the conjunction is ‘and’) ...though it may be _______ with diet and exercise. -> Use of though -> opposite of previous words -> This = less sense with Case 1, thus Case 1 = eliminated Summary -> Words = not different ; incurable ; (anything positive here) Option 1: Distinguishable ≠ not different -> Eliminated Option 2: Irreversible -> Not related to aging -> Out of scope -> Eliminated Option 4: Differentiated ≠ not different -> Eliminated Option 3: All words make sense -> Correct answer!
option1: distinguishable; permanent
option2: irreversible; manageable
option3: indistinguishable; reversible
option4: differentiated; visible

question: The new car has disappointed its buyers in many ways, and good mileage is its only ___________.
solution:
The blank needs an idiom meaning the only positive quality that redeems something otherwise disappointing. Option 1 -> saving grace -> means the one redeeming quality that makes up for other flaws. Option 2 -> saving praise -> not a standard idiom; grammatically incorrect construction. Option 3 -> saving case -> not a recognized idiom; doesn't fit the context. Option 4 -> saving face -> means avoiding embarrassment or maintaining dignity, not about redeeming qualities.
When something has many flaws but one good feature that makes it worthwhile, that feature is called a saving grace. It's like having a terrible movie with one amazing actor whose performance makes the whole thing bearable - that actor becomes the film's saving grace.
option1: saving grace
option2: saving praise
option3: saving case
option4: saving face

question: Apple’s challenge while opening physical stores is to
solution:
1. 'The real issue will be to pull consumers to these flagship centres without alienating the partner sellers.'
  2. This citation explains the challenge of retaining partner relations while attracting customers.
option1: provide stimulation that draws customers away from other sellers.
option2: retain links with their partner sellers while drawing customers to their stores.
option3: subtly alienate customers away from competitors.
option4: offer parallel products packaged more attractively.
comprehension:
Two Apple stores in Delhi and in Mumbai - opened in April 2023. Until now, Indians could buy Apple products in the country either online or through a vast network of what the company calls ‘premium resellers’. Will the new-found ability to sell products directly to customers in India - the world's second largest smartphone market - impact its fortunes here? Apple has long tried to open physical retail stores in India. In 2019, the Indian government relaxed some investment rules, exempting companies selling ‘cutting-edge’ items, such as Apple's iPhones and iPads, from restrictions.

Apple first launched an online store in India through a website which allows users to request for customised products. Apple’s decision to open physical stores in India could be the next step in its branding strategy. It is not as if this will change Apple’s sales in any dramatic way, but it is still a key milestone, one that will allow Apple to deliver to Indian customers the full Apple experience.

The iPhone is still an aspirational product in the price-sensitive Indian market. When you launch an Apple store, you are basically giving a premium experience to your premium consumers. It might not pull up sales, but it pulls more people into the Apple ecosystem.

Setting up an Apple store in India is also a statement that the country is now big enough for Apple to be actually interested in expanding operations here, and comes at a time when India is rising fast as a manufacturing base for the iPhone.

Apple first began making a lower-end model of the iPhone in India in 2017. Last year, it began producing its latest model of iPhone 14 in the country, which now accounts for 5 percent of total iPhone production.

For years, Apple had relied on China's sophisticated manufacturing network to produce the bulk of its products. But analysts predict that by 2025, a quarter of all iPhones the company makes could be produced in India. Apple was already making between 5-7 percent of its products in India, and they are targeting to go up to 25 percent of their manufacturing capacity.

In 2022, Apple had a 60 percent market share in the Indian ‘premium smartphone’ market - which refers to mobiles that cost INR 40,000 or more, ahead of Samsung's 21 percent share. Apple is doing well across categories. The real issue will be to pull consumers to these flagship centres without alienating the partner sellers.

Apple has finally understood that India’s premium market is growing, so why not be serious about it.

question: In the alleyways of old Cairo, in its cafes, warehouses and barbershops, framed portraits of Egyptian icons are _______ on fading walls. Kings, presidents, religious and political figures are all _______ as symbols of a glorious past.
solution: In old Cairo, portraits of Egyptian icons are _______ on fading walls. -> Use of ‘on’ -> Word will be related -> placement (Could be how many, could be how they were placed etc) Kings ... and political figures are all _______ as symbols of their glorious past. -> Use of ‘glorious’ -> Word = positive Option 1: Fashionable ≠ Placement -> Eliminated Option 3: Rare + Abundant = Mismatch -> eliminated Option 4: Substantial ≠ Placement -> Eliminated Option 2: All words make sense -> Correct answer!
option1: fashionable; confounded
option2: ubiquitous; immortalised
option3: rare; abundant
option4: substantial; dispersed

question: I <u>had been having a headache</u>, so I will not attend the meeting.
solution:
This question tests **tense consistency and cause-effect relationships**.

When explaining why you'll do something in the future, use present tense for current conditions. Think of it like: "I'm sick now, so I won't go tomorrow." The headache happening now justifies the future decision.

Option 1 → Missing "a" makes this grammatically incorrect, like saying "have cold."

Option 2 → Past tense doesn't explain a future decision - that headache's over!

Option 3 → Present tense perfectly explains why you won't attend later. ✓

Option 4 → Past habit doesn't justify future actions - sounds randomly nostalgic.
option1: have headache
option2: was having a headache
option3: have a headache
option4: used to have a headache

question: In my opinion, the psychological thrillers of Alfred Hitchcock, who is a master of suspense and horror, <u>have more lasting impact than any other filmmaker</u> in history.
solution:
This question tests subject-verb agreement and parallel comparison structures.

When comparing things, make sure your verb matches your subject in number, and compare apples to apples. "The thrillers have had impact" needs a plural verb, and we're comparing Hitchcock's thrillers to other filmmakers' thrillers (not the filmmakers themselves). Think of it like comparing your cooking skills to your friends' cooking skills, not to your friends directly!

Option 1 → Wrong tense and "any filmmaker" illogically includes Hitchcock himself.

Option 2 → "Are having" sounds awkward for describing lasting, ongoing impact.

Option 3 → "Has had" is singular but "thrillers" is plural—mismatch!

Option 4 → Perfect! "Have had" matches plural subject, compares correctly.
option1: had more lasting impact than any filmmaker
option2: are having more lasting impact than those of other filmmakers
option3: has had a more lasting impact than those of other filmmakers
option4: have had a more lasting impact than those of any other filmmaker

question: One of the statements below contains a word used incorrectly or inappropriately. Choose the option which has the incorrect or inappropriate usage of the word.
solution:
The question is testing for word usage between **proscribe** and **prescribe**. **Proscribe** means to forbid, ban, or prohibit something - like when your parents proscribe you from staying out past midnight, they're essentially putting a big "NO ENTRY" sign on late-night adventures. Think of it as the stricter cousin who always says "you can't do that!" **Prescribe** means to recommend, advise, or provide as a guideline - like when a doctor prescribes medicine or when a recipe prescribes specific ingredients. It's the helpful friend who tells you exactly what you should do to get the best results.
Option 1 -> Correct usage. The rules "forbid" or "prohibit" conflicts of interest. Option 2 -> Correct usage. The coach "forbids" or "bans" late night parties. Option 3 -> Incorrect usage. A manual doesn't "forbid" instructions - it "provides" or "prescribes" them. Option 4 -> Correct usage. The policy "prohibits" or "forbids" disclosure of confidential information.
Hence, Option 3 is correct
option1: The rules of the organization proscribes members from engaging in conflicts of interest.
option2: The coach proscribes late night parties before an important game.
option3: The software manual proscribes step-by-step instructions for installing and configuring the program.
option4: The company's policy proscribes employees from disclosing confidential information to unauthorized individuals.

question: Based on the information in the passage, which of the following statements best describes the potential danger associated with anxiety disorder?
solution: Option 4 is correct because the passage mentions anxiety disorders arise when danger is 'imagined rather than real, or is something that is exaggerated.' Options 1, 2, and 3 are incorrect as they misrepresent the nature of anxiety disorders described in the passage. Genetic factors are not stated as the primary cause, and not all anxiety indicates imagined danger, nor is it always proportionate to the real danger.
option1: It primarily arises from genetic factors rather than environmental influences.
option2: It is always indicative of an imagined danger rather than a real one.
option3: It occurs when the perceived danger is proportionate to the real danger.
option4: It is characterized by a persistent and exaggerated perception of danger.
comprehension:
Anxiety is an emotion that all people experience from time to time, and we do that for good reasons. It has been built into us; we have inherited it from our evolutionary past, because, in general, anxiety has a survival function. It kicks in whenever we sense danger – we freeze, we devote all of our attention to the danger, and our bodies react with a big release of adrenalin, an increase in blood flow to the muscles, getting us ready to run as fast as we can or fight as fiercely as we might. 

 Anxiety also helps us to focus on things when we have deadlines and, if someone is driving too fast when we cross the road, it helps us to jump out of the way quickly. So, there is nothing wrong with anxiety in general, and in fact, we would have difficulties if we did not experience it to some extent. However, it can get problematic if the danger is imagined rather than real, or is something that is exaggerated. In those cases, particularly if the perceived danger is out of proportion to the real danger, and it is persistent and disabling, then there is a danger of an anxiety disorder. 

 Scientists are looking at what role genes play in the development of these disorders and are also investigating the effects of environmental factors such as pollution, physical and psychological stress, and diet. Several parts of the brain are key actors in the production of fear and anxiety. Using brain imaging technology and neurochemical techniques, scientists have discovered that the amygdala plays a significant role in most anxiety disorders. By learning more about how the brain creates fear and anxiety, scientists may be able to devise better treatments for these disorders. 

 The first psychotherapy treatment that was shown to be effective was ‘exposure therapy’, which essentially encourages people in a graded way to go into their feared situations and stay in them for as long as they can and build their confidence that way. Another is ‘group cognitive behaviour therapy’, a talking therapy that helps people to understand the link between negative thoughts and mood and how altering their behaviour can enable them to manage anxiety and feel in control. There are, of course, drugs that can help people with anxiety, but medication will not cure an anxiety disorder, it can only help to keep it under control while the person receives psychotherapy. 

 There is plenty of evidence that exercise can help with anxiety problems. When stress affects the brain, with its many nerve connections, the rest of the body feels the impact as well. Exercise and other physical activity produce endorphins, which are chemicals in the brain that act as natural painkillers. Additionally, getting physically tired can help people fall asleep faster and have deeper, more relaxing sleep. As many people suffering from anxiety often have problems with insomnia, just the ability to get a good night’s rest can change their perspectives. 

 Anxiety is a normal but highly subjective human emotion. While normal anxiety serves a beneficial and adaptive purpose, it can also become the cause of tremendous suffering for millions of people. It is important that people recognise excessive anxiety in themselves at the earliest, as treatment can be very successful. Leaving it untreated can be a misery.

question: In today's fast-paced world, individuals are inundated with information, necessitating the cultivation of critical thinking skills in order to navigate the influx of data ______________. Additionally, the prevalence of fake news and misinformation presents a ______________ challenge, undermining people’s trust in media and institutions. Promoting media literacy and cultivating a culture of scepticism are vital for ______________ informed citizenship and democratic values.
solution: The focus is on critical thinking and its impact on dealing with information. 'Discerningly' implies a careful and insightful approach, matching the need for skillful handling of information. 'Pervasive' reflects the widespread challenge posed by misinformation, and 'fostering' directly connects to promoting literacy and scepticism, thus supporting the context perfectly.
option1: discerningly; pervasive; fostering
option2: thoughtfully; holistic; furthering
option3: discreetly; global; serving
option4: judiciously; systematic; inhibiting

question: The first sentence in the passage mentions that India was in the news again as a world leader. What is the writer’s intention in pointing this out?
solution: The correct answer is 'To be critical.' The sentence sets the tone for discussing a serious issue where India leads, but negatively, as it relates to pollution. The options 'To be negative,' 'To create confusion,' and 'To be judgmental' are incorrect as they imply an emotional reaction without the critical analytical context provided by the article.
option1: To be negative.
option2: To be critical.
option3: To create confusion.
option4: To be judgmental.
comprehension:
Recently, India was in the news again as a world leader – but this time for the wrong reason. According to the Swiss firm IQAir’s assessment of the air quality of world cities 2023, New Delhi is back to being the world’s most polluted capital. New Delhi’s annual air quality rating is 19 times the World Health Organization’s annual limit recommended in 2021 for its pollutant level. The picture is no better as far as the larger Union Territory of Delhi is considered – its annual record of pollutant levels makes it the third most polluted region globally. 

 Delhi may have hogged the headlines on air pollution, but the problem is far from unique to metropolises. IQAir’s findings reveal that even lower-tier cities and towns in India are choking because of very poor air quality. Indeed, Begusarai in Bihar is the world’s most polluted city. Partly industrialized, housing among others an oil refinery, Begusarai is primarily agrarian. And Begusarai is not an outlier: Mullanpur in Punjab, which is transitioning to an urban settlement, and Siwan in Bihar are among the 42 Indian cities/towns that are among the 50 most polluted globally. 

 Many judicial pronouncements have upheld “the right to clean air” as a corollary to the fundamental right to life and pushed governments to act. But Delhi’s bad air is despite the introduction of CNG in public transport and the building of a metro network in the early years of this decade, and in spite of the presence of a Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) that kicks in when the air quality turns bad and becomes more stringent as the air quality worsens. 

 Improving air quality across Indian cities requires three fundamental changes. One, governments must turn to science to understand, measure, and monitor the problem. Two, they have to understand that the response will have to include behavioural changes and use a combination of incentives and penalties to achieve this. And three, clean air has to be seen as a common public good. To be sure, it is not just the responsibility of the Centre or a battle for courts to wage, but a fight that states and municipal governments have to be a part of. This is the battle for our future.

question: The pursuit of a career in sports offers individuals opportunities for personal fulfilment and professional success. ______________________________. Athletes must therefore undergo rigorous training regimens to maintain peak physical condition and hone their skills. Additionally, competition in the sports industry is fierce, requiring dedication, perseverance, and mental resilience to succeed. Despite the challenges, successful athletes can enjoy lucrative contracts, endorsements, and global recognition for their achievements.
solution: The best approach here is to look for a sentence that links the opportunities and challenges mentioned in the paragraph. Option 1 smoothly transitions from the opportunities offered by sports to the reasons for rigorous training by discussing the short-lived nature of such careers, which makes it the most relevant and fitting completion. The other options, while plausible, don't provide a compelling link between opportunities for fulfilment and the need for rigorous training.
option1: Sports careers are often short-lived due to the physical demands and risks of injury associated with athletic competition
option2: Many athletes choose to pursue higher education alongside their sports careers to ensure long-term stability and success
option3: The sports industry encompasses a wide range of career paths, including coaching, sports management, and sports journalism
option4: Athletes often face pressure from coaches, sponsors, and fans to perform at their best, adding to the stress of professional sports

question: Conspiracy theorists suggest that if climate change is slowly turning the planet into an oven, then this must have been the outcome of our enemies’ intentional ______________. If there are no coincidences in the world, then everything, including ______________ events, can be controlled. It is only a matter of power and ______________.
solution: To approach this, recognize the sinister tone implied by 'conspiracy theorists' and 'enemies’. The word 'plotting' fits into this context better than 'errors', 'machinations', or 'inaction', as it implies intentional planning. 'Catastrophic' aligns with the severity suggested by the climate turning into an 'oven', and 'prowess' denotes capability or power, which is a fitting conclusion to the statement about control.
option1: errors; indescribable; information
option2: machinations; outstanding; determination
option3: plotting; catastrophic; prowess
option4: inaction; advantageous; performance

comprehension:

But Delhi’s bad air is despite the introduction of CNG in public transport and the building of a metro network in the early years of this decade, and in spite of the presence of a Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) that kicks in when the air quality turns bad and becomes more stringent as the air quality worsens. 

 Improving air quality across Indian cities requires three fundamental changes. One, governments must turn to science to understand, measure, and monitor the problem. Two, they have to understand that the response will have to include behavioural changes and use a combination of incentives and penalties to achieve this. And three, clean air has to be seen as a common public good. To be sure, it is not just the responsibility of the Centre or a battle for courts to wage, but a fight that states and municipal governments have to be a part of. This is the battle for our future.
question: Based on the information in the passage, which one of the following statements is correct?
solution:
We need the statement that is accurately supported by the passage.

Option A -> Incorrect. The passage states that New Delhi is the most polluted capital, not the most polluted city. Begusarai is the most polluted city.

Option B -> Incorrect. The passage explicitly says that even lower-tier cities and towns suffer from severe air pollution, so the problem is not limited to major cities.

Option C -> Incorrect. The passage states that 42 Indian cities/towns are among the 50 most polluted globally, not all 50.

Option D -> Correct. The passage clearly says, “Begusarai in Bihar is the world’s most polluted city.” This matches the option exactly with no assumptions required.

Therefore, Option D is correct.
option1: Delhi’s air quality is the poorest in the entire world.
option2: The problem of air pollution is limited to the major cities in India.
option3: The 50 most polluted cities in the world are in India.
option4: Industrialization makes Begusarai the most polluted city in the world.

question: One of the statements below contains a word used incorrectly or inappropriately. Choose the option which has the incorrect or inappropriate usage of the word.
solution:
The question is testing for word usage of **stay**. **Stay** means to remain in a particular place, condition, or position without moving or changing. Think of it like when you tell your dog to "stay" - you want them to remain in that exact spot! It can also mean to continue in a certain state (like staying awake) or to reside temporarily somewhere (like staying at a hotel).
Option 1 -> Correct usage. "Stay awake" means to remain in the state of being awake. Option 2 -> Incorrect usage. "Stay away" means to avoid or keep at a distance from something, but the sentence describes cattle actually wandering INTO fields and eating crops - they're not staying away, they're actively going places. Option 3 -> Correct usage. "Stay order" is a legal term meaning a temporary halt or suspension of proceedings. Option 4 -> Correct usage. "Stay with" means to reside temporarily as a guest.
Hence, Option 2 is correct
option1: She said, “I always stay awake late into the night till my daughters come home, as I worry a lot about their safety.”
option2: Due to the ban on slaughter of cattle, the neglected ones often stay away, wandering into fields and eating the crops.
option3: The ownership of the property went to the eldest son of the family, but the other siblings went to court and asked for a stay order.
option4: During her summer holidays she usually went to the village to stay with her grandparents and her large extended family.

question: The passage is chiefly concerned with
solution: Option 4 is correct as the passage comprehensively discusses the role of anxiety from an evolutionary perspective and its various impacts and treatments. Options 1, 2, and 3, while relevant to aspects of anxiety treatment or causes, do not represent the primary focus, which is more broadly on anxiety's nature and purpose.
option1: evaluating the effectiveness of psychotherapy and medication in treating anxiety.
option2: investigating the physiological benefits of exercise on anxiety management.
option3: understanding the complex interplay between genetic and environmental factors in anxiety disorders.
option4: exploring the multifaceted nature of anxiety and its adaptive functions.

By learning more about how the brain creates fear and anxiety, scientists may be able to devise better treatments for these disorders.

Another is ‘group cognitive behaviour therapy’, a talking therapy that helps people to understand the link between negative thoughts and mood and how altering their behaviour can enable them to manage anxiety and feel in control. There are, of course, drugs that can help people with anxiety, but medication will not cure an anxiety disorder, it can only help to keep it under control while the person receives psychotherapy.

Exercise and other physical activity produce endorphins, which are chemicals in the brain that act as natural painkillers. Additionally, getting physically tired can help people fall asleep faster and have deeper, more relaxing sleep. As many people suffering from anxiety often have problems with insomnia, just the ability to get a good night’s rest can change their perspectives.

It is important that people recognise excessive anxiety in themselves at the earliest, as treatment can be very successful. Leaving it untreated can be a misery.

question: All the employees agreed that the new regulations had __________ positive changes in the workplace.
solution:
The blank needs a phrasal verb meaning to cause or create positive changes. Option 1 -> brought about -> means to cause or create something, typically changes or results. Option 2 -> brought on -> means to cause something negative like illness or problems, not positive changes. Option 3 -> brought along -> means to take someone or something with you when going somewhere. Option 4 -> brought up -> means to raise a child or mention a topic in conversation.
When a new policy brings about positive changes in your workplace, it means those changes didn't just happen randomly - the policy actually caused them to occur. It's like planting seeds that grow into the improvements everyone wanted to see.
option1: brought about
option2: brought on
option3: brought along
option4: brought up

question:
The sentences given below, when properly sequenced, form a coherent paragraph. Each sentence is labelled with a number. Decide on the most logical order and enter the sequence of numbers in the space provided.
  1. Farmers, however, especially during the peak farming season, ignore the warnings of the weather forecast.
  2. People from some states of India have demanded that families of those who have been killed by lightning strikes should receive compensation.
  3. The Minister for Disaster Management announced that over 2800 people have died after being struck by a lightning last year.
  4. The Minister responded that pamphlets on the dangers of lightning and precautions to be taken are being distributed in villages.
solution:
The passage talks about lightning deaths and government response measures. Link A (3 → 2): Sentence 2's "those who have been killed by lightning strikes" specifically references the 2800 lightning deaths announced in sentence 3. Link B (2 → 4): "The Minister responded" in sentence 4 directly addresses the compensation demands mentioned in sentence 2. Link C (4 → 1): "However" in sentence 1 contrasts farmers ignoring warnings against the Minister's pamphlet distribution efforts from sentence 4.
Introduction: Sentence 3 is the introducing sentence because it provides the key statistical announcement about lightning deaths. Conclusion: Sentence 1 is the concluding sentence because it explains why the problem persists despite government efforts.
Assembling: Minister announces deaths, people demand compensation, Minister responds with pamphlets, but farmers ignore warnings anyway. Hence the correct order is 3241

question: The negotiations are unsuccessful, and the <u>Boards’ hopes to continuing talks on a future deal seems</u> to be increasingly improbable.
solution:
This question tests subject-verb agreement and infinitive construction.

Subject-verb agreement means the verb must match its subject in number (singular/plural). When using infinitives, we pair "to" with the base form of the verb, like "to continue" not "to continuing." Think of it like a dance - the subject and verb need to move in sync, and infinitives follow a strict two-step pattern.

Option 1 → Perfect grammar tango: singular board, plural hopes, correct infinitive.

Option 2 → Wrong verb agreement and mangled infinitive - double grammar fail.

Option 3 → Awkward "hopes on continued" phrasing disrupts the smooth flow.

Option 4 → Triple trouble: wrong possessive, wrong infinitive, wrong verb agreement.
option1: Board’s hopes to continue talks on a future deal seem
option2: Board’s hopes to continuing talks on a future deal seems
option3: Boards’ hopes on continued talks on a future deal seem
option4: Boards’ hopes to continued talks on a future deal seems

question: My supervisor seemed to be in a bad mood because he __________ when I asked if I could leave an hour early.
solution:
The blank needs an idiom meaning to respond angrily or harshly to someone. Option 1 -> cut my voice -> not a standard English idiom; doesn't convey angry response. Option 2 -> ate my head -> not a recognized English expression; grammatically incorrect. Option 3 -> hit my ear off -> not a standard idiom; doesn't make logical sense. Option 4 -> bit my head off -> means to respond very angrily or harshly to someone .
When someone bites your head off, they respond to you with unexpected anger or harshness, often over something minor. It's like a verbal attack that catches you off guard - one moment you're asking a simple question, and the next moment they're snapping at you like an angry animal.
option1: cut my voice
option2: ate my head
option3: hit my ear off
option4: bit my head off

question: In what way might the relationship between exercise or physical activity and anxiety relief suggest a potential solution for individuals with anxiety disorders?
solution: Option 3 is correct as the passage notes that getting physically tired can help improve sleep quality, which in turn can relieve anxiety. Options 1 and 4 are incorrect as there is no direct link mentioned between endorphins enhancing cognitive therapy or targeting the amygdala. Option 2 is partially true but overlooks the long-term benefits mentioned like improved sleep.
option1: Exercise-induced endorphins facilitate cognitive behavioural therapy’s effectiveness.
option2: Physical activity serves as a temporary distraction from anxiety, but its long-term effects remain unclear.
option3: Physical activity could enhance sleep quality, indirectly lessening anxiety symptoms.
option4: Exercise may directly target the amygdala to reduce anxiety symptoms.
comprehension:
Anxiety is an emotion that all people experience from time to time, and we do that for good reasons. It has been built into us; we have inherited it from our evolutionary past, because, in general, anxiety has a survival function. It kicks in whenever we sense danger – we freeze, we devote all of our attention to the danger, and our bodies react with a big release of adrenalin, an increase in blood flow to the muscles, getting us ready to run as fast as we can or fight as fiercely as we might. 

 Anxiety also helps us to focus on things when we have deadlines and, if someone is driving too fast when we cross the road, it helps us to jump out of the way quickly. So, there is nothing wrong with anxiety in general, and in fact, we would have difficulties if we did not experience it to some extent. However, it can get problematic if the danger is imagined rather than real, or is something that is exaggerated. In those cases, particularly if the perceived danger is out of proportion to the real danger, and it is persistent and disabling, then there is a danger of an anxiety disorder. 

 Scientists are looking at what role genes play in the development of these disorders and are also investigating the effects of environmental factors such as pollution, physical and psychological stress, and diet. Several parts of the brain are key actors in the production of fear and anxiety. Using brain imaging technology and neurochemical techniques, scientists have discovered that the amygdala plays a significant role in most anxiety disorders. By learning more about how the brain creates fear and anxiety, scientists may be able to devise better treatments for these disorders. 

 The first psychotherapy treatment that was shown to be effective was ‘exposure therapy’, which essentially encourages people in a graded way to go into their feared situations and stay in them for as long as they can and build their confidence that way. Another is ‘group cognitive behaviour therapy’, a talking therapy that helps people to understand the link between negative thoughts and mood and how altering their behaviour can enable them to manage anxiety and feel in control. There are, of course, drugs that can help people with anxiety, but medication will not cure an anxiety disorder, it can only help to keep it under control while the person receives psychotherapy. 

 There is plenty of evidence that exercise can help with anxiety problems. When stress affects the brain, with its many nerve connections, the rest of the body feels the impact as well. Exercise and other physical activity produce endorphins, which are chemicals in the brain that act as natural painkillers. Additionally, getting physically tired can help people fall asleep faster and have deeper, more relaxing sleep. As many people suffering from anxiety often have problems with insomnia, just the ability to get a good night’s rest can change their perspectives. 

 Anxiety is a normal but highly subjective human emotion. While normal anxiety serves a beneficial and adaptive purpose, it can also become the cause of tremendous suffering for millions of people. It is important that people recognise excessive anxiety in themselves at the earliest, as treatment can be very successful. Leaving it untreated can be a misery.

question: Anil could not go to his friend’s party, as he had fever and was __________.
solution:
The blank needs an idiom meaning to feel sick or unwell. Option 1 -> beneath the weather -> incorrect preposition; this is not a standard idiom. Option 2 -> under the weather -> means feeling sick or unwell, which fits the context perfectly. Option 3 -> beside the weather -> incorrect preposition; this is not a standard idiom. Option 4 -> outside the weather -> incorrect preposition; this is not a standard idiom.
When you're feeling a bit sick or not quite yourself, you can say you're under the weather. It's like the weather is pressing down on you, making you feel sluggish and unwell - perfect for those days when you need to stay home with a fever instead of going out to have fun.
option1: beneath the weather
option2: under the weather
option3: beside the weather
option4: outside the weather

question: Based on the information in the passage, who has endorsed the right to clean air?
solution: The correct answer is 'The courts.' The passage explicitly mentions judicial pronouncements upholding the right to clean air. The media, government, and parliament are not mentioned as endorsers of this right in the passage.
option1: The media.
option2: The government.
option3: The courts.
option4: Parliament.
comprehension:
Recently, India was in the news again as a world leader – but this time for the wrong reason. According to the Swiss firm IQAir’s assessment of the air quality of world cities 2023, New Delhi is back to being the world’s most polluted capital. New Delhi’s annual air quality rating is 19 times the World Health Organization’s annual limit recommended in 2021 for its pollutant level. The picture is no better as far as the larger Union Territory of Delhi is considered – its annual record of pollutant levels makes it the third most polluted region globally. 

 Delhi may have hogged the headlines on air pollution, but the problem is far from unique to metropolises. IQAir’s findings reveal that even lower-tier cities and towns in India are choking because of very poor air quality. Indeed, Begusarai in Bihar is the world’s most polluted city. Partly industrialized, housing among others an oil refinery, Begusarai is primarily agrarian. And Begusarai is not an outlier: Mullanpur in Punjab, which is transitioning to an urban settlement, and Siwan in Bihar are among the 42 Indian cities/towns that are among the 50 most polluted globally. 

 Many judicial pronouncements have upheld “the right to clean air” as a corollary to the fundamental right to life and pushed governments to act. But Delhi’s bad air is despite the introduction of CNG in public transport and the building of a metro network in the early years of this decade, and in spite of the presence of a Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) that kicks in when the air quality turns bad and becomes more stringent as the air quality worsens. 

 Improving air quality across Indian cities requires three fundamental changes. One, governments must turn to science to understand, measure, and monitor the problem. Two, they have to understand that the response will have to include behavioural changes and use a combination of incentives and penalties to achieve this. And three, clean air has to be seen as a common public good. To be sure, it is not just the responsibility of the Centre or a battle for courts to wage, but a fight that states and municipal governments have to be a part of. This is the battle for our future.

question: Which of the following points is not made by the writer?
solution:
From the passage: The writer argues for science-based approaches, behaviour change backed by incentives and penalties, and treating clean air as a shared public good. They also stress that air quality improvement requires coordination between the Centre, states, and municipal bodies.
Option 1 → Governments at all levels should be involved in tackling the problem of air pollution → Incorrect The writer clearly says this is a multi-level responsibility. They make it explicit that the Centre alone cannot fix the problem and that state and municipal governments must be involved. So this point is made. Option 2 → Scientific methods are needed to address the problem of air pollution → Incorrect The writer lists “turning to science to understand, measure, and monitor the problem” as one of the three fundamental changes required. This means scientific methods are central to their proposed solution. So the point is made. Option 3 → Polluting behaviour should be punished → Incorrect The author argues that improving air quality requires behavioural change supported by both incentives and penalties. Penalties imply consequences or punishments for harmful actions. Therefore, the idea that polluting behaviour should face penalties is indeed part of the author’s argument. Option 4 → Clean air is the right of every consumer → Correct The author speaks about clean air as a public good and as part of the right to life, but never frames it as a “right of consumers.” That wording reflects a market-based or transactional perspective that the author does not use. Thus, this is the only statement the writer does not make.
Therefore, Option 4 is correct.
option1: Governments at all levels should be involved in tackling the problem of air pollution.
option2: Scientific methods are needed to address the problem of air pollution.
option3: Polluting behaviour should be punished.
option4: Clean air is the right of every consumer.

question:
The sentences given below, when properly sequenced, form a coherent paragraph. Each sentence is labelled with a number. Decide on the most logical order and enter the sequence of numbers in the space provided.
  1. Meanwhile, people are posting less. The share of Americans who say they enjoy documenting their life online has fallen from 40 per cent to 28 per cent since 2020.
  2. The striking feature of the new social media is that they are no longer very social.
  3. Some of the consequences of this are welcome. Political campaigners say they have to tone down their message to win over private groups.
  4. Debate is moving to closed platforms such as WhatsApp and Telegram.
  5. Inspired by TikTok, apps like Facebook increasingly serve a diet of clips selected by artificial intelligence according to a user’s viewing behaviour, not their social connections
solution:
The passage talks about how social media platforms are becoming less social Link A (2 → 5): Sentence 2 introduces that social media is "no longer very social," and sentence 5 explains why through AI-selected content replacing social connections. Link B (5 → 1): "Meanwhile" in sentence 1 shows a parallel trend happening alongside the AI-driven content shift described in sentence 5. Link C (1 → 4): Both sentences describe the movement away from public social media engagement toward private platforms. Link D (4 → 3): "Some of the consequences of this" in sentence 3 specifically refers back to the closed platform movement mentioned in sentence 4.
Introduction: Sentence 2 is the introducing sentence because it presents the main thesis about social media's declining social nature. Conclusion: Sentence 3 is the concluding sentence because it discusses the consequences and implications of the described trends.
Assembling: Starting with the thesis, explaining the cause, showing parallel trends, then describing consequences of the shift. Hence the correct order is 25143

question: In his address to the candidates contesting the student council elections the Principal said, “If you want others to take you seriously then you must __________ on issues like gender bias and inclusivity.”
solution:
The blank needs an idiom meaning to practice what you preach or act according to your stated beliefs. Option 1 -> rule the roost -> means to be in charge or dominate, not about matching actions with words. Option 2 -> go around the bend -> means to go crazy or lose one's mind, unrelated to the context. Option 3 -> walk the talk -> means to act in accordance with what you say or believe, which fits perfectly. Option 4 -> read the lines -> means to understand the obvious meaning, not about taking action on beliefs.
When someone tells you to walk the talk, they're essentially saying "put your money where your mouth is." It means actually doing what you say you believe in, rather than just talking about it. If you constantly preach about environmental protection but drive a gas-guzzling car, you're definitely not walking the talk!
option1: rule the roost
option2: go around the bend
option3: walk the talk
option4: read the lines

question: In the last sentence of the passage, 'This is the battle for our future', the writer is
solution: The correct answer is 'emphasizing the need to fight for clean air.' This statement underscores the urgency and importance of combating air pollution. The options 'being ambivalent,' 'being melodramatic,' and 'projecting a bleak future' do not accurately reflect the proactive and determined tone of the statement.
option1: being ambivalent.
option2: being melodramatic.
option3: emphasizing the need to fight for clean air.
option4: projecting a bleak future for India.

question: Based on the information in the passage, which one of the following statements is correct?
solution: Option 1 is correct as the passage specifically mentions exposure therapy as a graded method to face feared situations. Options 2 and 3 are incorrect as the passage clearly supports the benefits of exercise and states that medication is not a cure but a control method. Option 4, while plausible, is not explicitly stated as the most preferred treatment.
option1: Exposure therapy encourages individuals to face their feared situations gradually.
option2: Regular exercise does little to alleviate anxiety symptoms and improve sleep quality.
option3: Medication is the first-line therapy and primary cure for anxiety disorders.
option4: Cognitive behaviour therapy is the most preferred treatment for anxiety disorders.

question: In a circus where performers share dressing rooms, training spaces and work in a fast-paced environment, the success of a show __________ fostering team-work and collaboration.
solution:
The blank needs a phrasal verb meaning "depends on" or "is fundamentally about." Option 1 -> comes down to -> means "is fundamentally about" or "depends on" - fits perfectly. Option 2 -> comes about -> means "happens" or "occurs" - doesn't fit the context. Option 3 -> come along with -> means "accompanies" - wrong subject-verb agreement and meaning. Option 4 -> comes with -> means "includes" or "is accompanied by" - doesn't convey dependency.
When something comes down to a particular factor, it means that factor is the most important element that determines success or failure. Like when a basketball game comes down to the final shot, or when getting a job comes down to having the right experience - it's the crucial deciding factor that makes all the difference.
option1: comes down to
option2: comes about
option3: come along with
option4: comes with

question: He is very careless and <u>has loosed his keys</u> all the time.
solution:
This question tests verb forms and tense usage for habitual actions.

When describing habits or repeated actions (like "all the time"), we use simple present tense. Also, don't confuse "lose" (to misplace) with "loose" (not tight) - they're completely different words!

Option 1 → Wrong verb and wrong tense - "loosing" isn't even a real word.

Option 2 → You can't mix "has" with simple present "loses" - grammar chaos!

Option 3 → Past continuous doesn't work for ongoing habits happening "all the time."

Option 4 → Perfect! Simple present tense for his annoying key-losing habit.
option1: is loosing his keys
option2: has loses his keys
option3: was losing his keys
option4: loses his keys

question: The students’ picnic plans __________ when it started raining heavily in the morning.
solution:
The blank needs a phrasal verb meaning to fail or be cancelled unexpectedly. Option 1 -> dropped out -> means to withdraw from something voluntarily, not about plans failing. Option 2 -> dropped off -> means to decrease gradually or to deliver something, not about plans failing. Option 3 -> fell behind -> means to lag or not keep up with schedule, not about complete failure. Option 4 -> fell through -> means to fail to happen or be cancelled, especially when something was planned.
When your carefully planned weekend trip to the beach suddenly falls through because of bad weather, it means your plans have completely collapsed or failed to materialize. It's like watching a house of cards tumble down - everything you arranged just doesn't happen as expected.
option1: dropped out
option2: dropped off
option3: fell behind
option4: fell through

question: ______________________________. This happened after a lorry laden with tomatoes worth 21 lakh rupees went missing. One of the three traders who had sent his tomatoes on the lorry said that they had lost contact with the driver in Nashik and that his GPS had been switched off. The traders were not sure if the truck had been hijacked or if the driver had stolen the tomatoes. This is the third time tomatoes have been stolen following the rise of tomato prices to Rs.150 per kg.
solution: To determine the best fit, the missing sentence should ideally set the context for the subsequent detailed events. Option 1 effectively introduces the atmosphere of panic and confusion, which is consistent with the incident of a missing lorry described immediately after. The other options either jump into specifics unrelated to the ensuing details (Option 2 and 4) or provide irrelevant background information (Option 3).
option1: Panic and confusion reigned among traders in the Kolar agricultural produce market
option2: A couple took the hijacked vehicle to a nearby market and sold the tomatoes there
option3: Nashik overtook Kolar as the biggest tomato market in Asia
option4: Three traders jointly hired a lorry to carry tomatoes

question: It is widely argued that social media can benefit aspiring ______________. Populists in particular latch on to social media today as a way to connect directly with people, ______________ restraints on their behaviour that political parties would have provided in the pre-internet age. They can also profit from echo chambers, which reinforce the sense that a whole people ______________ supports a populist leader.
solution: Considering the description of bypassing traditional political restraints and leveraging echo chambers, 'autocrats' fits as it denotes authoritarian figures who might benefit from such direct and unmoderated influence. 'Bypassing' is a direct action that fits with avoiding traditional controls, and 'uniformly' supports the notion of widespread support within echo chambers.
option1: entrepreneurs; enforcing; presumably
option2: democrats; avoiding; surprisingly
option3: autocrats; bypassing; uniformly
option4: anarchists; unscrupulous; unanimously

question: In an era where privacy and individualism are often prized above all else, Japanese architect Riken Yamamoto blurs the boundary between public and private. ______________________________. His work is a testament to the power of architecture to break down barriers and to foster a sense of community. Through his innovative designs, he challenges traditional notions of public and private spaces, seamlessly blending the two realms.
solution: The missing sentence should link Yamamoto's architectural philosophy with the impact of his work. Option 4 is the most effective as it captures the essence of blurring public and private boundaries and his role as a 'visionary voice' promoting community through architectural design. Options 1 and 3 describe aspects of his work but don't connect as strongly to the theme of communal living, and Option 2 focuses on an award, which is irrelevant to the topic.
option1: He is deeply committed to building harmonious societies through his architectural practice
option2: He was recently named the 2024 Laureate of the prestigious Pritzker Architecture Prize
option3: He successfully integrates traditional Japanese aesthetics and modern design principles in his work
option4: In the world of architecture, he stands out as a visionary voice advocating a return to a more interconnected way of living

question: Sheila keeps interrupting Manohar when he is playing with his friends, but he __________ because she is his younger sister.
solution:
The blank needs a phrasal verb meaning to tolerate or endure someone's annoying behavior. Option 1 -> puts up with her -> means to tolerate or endure someone despite their annoying behavior. Option 2 -> puts along with her -> incorrect preposition; 'puts along with' is not a standard phrasal verb. Option 3 -> puts her off -> means to postpone something or to make someone lose interest, not to tolerate. Option 4 -> puts her on -> means to tease someone or pretend something is true, not to tolerate behavior.
When your annoying little brother keeps bothering you while you're trying to study, but you don't get angry because you love him anyway, you put up with his behavior. It means tolerating someone or something unpleasant because you have to or choose to, even when it tests your patience.
option1: puts up with her
option2: puts along with her
option3: puts her off
option4: puts her on

question: One of the statements below contains a word used incorrectly or inappropriately. Choose the option which has the incorrect or inappropriate usage of the word.
solution:
The question is testing for word usage between **train** and **trail**. A **train** can refer to a railway vehicle, or metaphorically to a sequence of connected thoughts, events, or things that follow one after another - like when you lose your "train of thought" during a conversation and can't remember what you were going to say next. A **trail** is a path or track left behind by someone or something that has passed by. Think of it like breadcrumbs in a fairy tale - if you're hiking and drop pieces of food behind you, you're leaving a trail that others can follow to find you.
Option 1 -> "Train of thought" is correct - refers to a sequence of connected ideas. Option 2 -> "Train" is correct - means to teach skills or behavior to the dog. Option 3 -> "Train" is correct - refers to railway transportation. Option 4 -> "Train of blood" is incorrect - should be "trail of blood" (a path of blood drops to follow).
Hence, Option 4 is correct
option1: The sound of laughter and loud music from the neighbour’s house disturbed his train of thought as he was writing his farewell speech.
option2: My friend has picked up a stray dog from the street and is now looking for somebody to train and domesticate the animal.
option3: If you really want to see the landscape and the scenic countryside, it is better to take a train or go by road, rather than by plane.
option4: By following the train of blood on the platform the police located the body of a young woman who had been brutally stabbed.

question: An ______________ found that one of the marathon runners had ______________ slowed down in the last two kilometres and after ______________ to the one behind him had let him cross the finish line just one second before he did.
solution: The word 'investigation' implies a thorough inquiry, suitable for discovering facts about the race. 'Deliberately' indicates intentional action, aligning with the scenario where a runner slows down purposefully. 'Signalling' to another runner suggests a communicative gesture, fitting the description of letting someone else win.
option1: analysis; systematically; waving
option2: appraisal; particularly; speaking
option3: overview; purposely; beckoning
option4: investigation; deliberately; signalling

question: In an era of globalization, cultural diversity is celebrated and valued across societies. However, the rise of xenophobia and intolerance poses a ______________ challenge to social cohesion and harmony. Building inclusive communities and promoting intercultural dialogue are essential for ______________ mutual understanding and respect among diverse populations. Additionally, educating individuals about the benefits of cultural diversity and challenging ______________ are crucial steps toward creating inclusive societies and encouraging peaceful coexistence.
solution: 'Pressing' suggests urgency, which is apt given the negative impacts of xenophobia. 'Nurturing' mutual understanding aligns with fostering positive intercultural relationships. 'Stereotypes' are commonly challenged in discussions of diversity, making this option the most coherent and contextually appropriate choice.
option1: multi-faceted; stimulating; archetypes
option2: pressing; nurturing; stereotypes
option3: sophisticated; expediting; propositions
option4: complicated; advancing; truisms

question: The phrase 'that kicks in' (para 3) means
solution: The correct answer is 'comes into force.' It refers to the Graded Response Action Plan activating under certain conditions. The other options do not fit the context of the passage where a response plan is implemented.
option1: comes into force.
option2: causes violence.
option3: gains importance.
option4: gets rejected.

question: Which of the following best indicates the relationship between anxiety and survival?
solution: Option 4 is correct because the passage explains that anxiety has a survival function inherited from our evolutionary past. Options 1, 2, and 3 misinterpret this role, suggesting either limited relevance, disruption to thriving, or frequent undermining of survival, none of which align with the passage's description.
option1: Anxiety is part of our evolutionary past, yet has limited relevance now as it does not significantly impact our chances of survival.
option2: Anxiety, an intrinsic part of human nature, often disrupts our ability to thrive by acting as an impediment to survival.
option3: Despite being inherited from our evolutionary past, anxiety frequently undermines survival as it is not an integral part of human nature.
option4: Anxiety is ingrained within us due to our evolutionary heritage and generally serves to promote survival.
